extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu survived a long solo breakaway to win by 10 seconds after POGAČAR attacked late on the finishing circuit walls, gaining crucial time on VAN AERT.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR used late circuit attacks to test rivals' legs after 200km, nearly catching solo leader while gaining GC time.
VAN DER POEL measured effort in final 300m on rough surface rather than sprinting, protecting time gap through technical finish.
FORMOLO Davide support work enabled POGAČAR's late attack effectiveness, team positioning before finale walls proved decisive.

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: managed 200km+ solo effort to exhaustion but held pacing discipline through technical finale to preserve narrow victory
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: attacked when VAN AERT showed weakness, gained 39 seconds on main GC rival while nearly catching breakaway
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: unable to respond to POGAČAR's attack on circuit walls, lost crucial 39 seconds and now 1:15 down GC
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: moved to third overall, suggesting strong climbing form on repeated circuit walls
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: jumped from 11th to 4th GC, indicating selective aggression on finale suited his climbing ability
rider_signals.FORMOLO Davide: positive: key domestique work setting up POGAČAR's attack, support role execution on finale circuit
